category_title: Students who withdraw or read unsanctioned texts to resist remedial instruction
student_description: This student exhibits task resistance (refusing to read, reading unsanctioned texts) because they feel demeaned by remedial, 'babyish' materials. They are highly capable and uniquely need intellectual stimulation, substantive texts, and tasks framed as high-level critical thinking rather than basic skill compliance.

criteria.rationale: The student feels 'mad n stupid' when forced to read 'skinny' remedial books, so long-term engagement requires complex, age-appropriate literature.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term plan explicitly incorporates substantive, complex texts (e.g., YA literature, novels) and abandons prescribed 'babyish' remedial materials.

criteria.rationale: Basic skill-building and memorization bore this student, whereas deep disciplinary inquiry sustains their interest over time.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term strategy shifts the focus away from basic skill-building toward high-level disciplinary inquiry, such as analyzing multiple perspectives or making text-based claims.
